notes: Country-level VIZA RAG seed for belgium. This file is the source of truth for this country's visitor/tourism visa knowledge and is designed to evolve alongside the country-specific form-filling workflow.

chunks.title: Chinese passport tourism route for Belgium
chunks.content: Chinese ordinary passport holders planning a short tourism trip to Belgium should normally use the Belgium/Schengen short-stay Type C tourism route when Belgium is the only destination, the longest-stay destination, or the main purpose destination. VIZA AI should first confirm nationality, legal residence, destination set, stay length, day split across Schengen countries, first entry if stays are equal, purpose, passport validity, travel medical insurance, accommodation, itinerary, funds, return ties and biometrics. Do not route this trip to US, UK, Vietnam, Indonesia or another non-Schengen visa.

chunks.title: Belgium Schengen where-to-apply and documents
chunks.content: EU Schengen guidance says a short-stay Schengen visa covers temporary visits of up to 90 days in any 180-day period. For a single-country trip, apply to the consulate/official visa channel of that country. For multiple Schengen countries, apply to the country where the traveller will spend the longest time or, if stays are equal, the first-entry country. Applications should normally be submitted at least 15 days before travel and no earlier than 6 months before the intended journey. Common documents include passport, signed Schengen application form, ICAO-compliant photo, travel medical insurance, itinerary/accommodation, funds, evidence of return intention, and fingerprints. EU guidance lists the adult visa fee as EUR 90 and normal processing as 15 days, extendable up to 45 days. Use Belgium's official consular or outsourced visa application channel for the applicant's legal residence country before booking an appointment. EU common source: https://home-affairs.ec.europa.eu/policies/schengen-borders-and-visa/visa-policy/applying-schengen-visa_en

documents.title: Belgium Application Form and Document Intake Requirements

chunks.title: Belgium application channel and form scope
chunks.content: Use the competent Belgium consulate or its authorised visa application centre when Belgium is the only destination, main destination, longest-stay destination, or first-entry state when stays are equal. Also check the country-specific source: https://dofi.ibz.be/en/themes/third-country-nationals/short-stay. Official source URLs reviewed for this seed: https://home-affairs.ec.europa.eu/document/download/5fe79baa-2d46-4b65-84ec-ea2e9b5ab1d6_en?filename=Visa+Application+Form.pdf ; https://dofi.ibz.be/en/themes/third-country-nationals/short-stay. This RAG chunk is for visitor/tourism-oriented intake and form-filling preparation; route work, study, residence, immigration, journalism or other regulated purposes to a separate country workflow.

chunks.title: Belgium fields to collect before filling the form
chunks.content: The harmonised Schengen form intake should collect surname/current and birth names, first names, date/place/country of birth, current and birth nationality, sex, civil status, parental authority for minors, national ID where applicable, travel document type/number/issue/expiry/issuing authority, home address/email/phone, residence in another country if any, occupation, employer or school, travel purpose, destination Member State(s), first-entry Member State, number of entries requested, intended arrival/departure dates and duration, prior Schengen fingerprints, entry permit for final destination if any, inviting person/company or hotel/accommodation, who pays travel/living costs, EU/EEA/Swiss family member data if applicable, and applicant declaration/signature.

chunks.title: Belgium supporting documents and review checklist
chunks.content: Core Schengen evidence includes signed visa application form, passport/travel document valid beyond the planned Schengen exit, photo, travel medical insurance, proof of purpose, itinerary/transport, accommodation or host invitation, evidence of financial means, evidence of intention to return, residence/jurisdiction proof, fee, and biometrics where required. Workflow guardrails: VIZA should determine the competent Schengen state before filling forms. For multi-country trips, compute longest stay or first entry if equal; collect day split and accommodation per country. Do not submit or sign declarations without explicit user confirmation.

chunks.title: Standard passport identity-field answer rules
chunks.content: Standard-answer identity fields must be answered from the applicant's passport biodata page, MRZ, official identity document, or the official dropdown options. This applies to passport number, name, date of birth, sex, nationality, issuing country, issue date, expiry date, issuing authority, place of issue, and passport/document type. For issuing authority or place-of-issue questions, do not infer the answer from the pickup city, residence city, application country, or travel destination; copy the exact Authority / Issuing authority / Place of issue wording printed on the passport if present. For Chinese ordinary passports, newer valid passports may show National Immigration Administration, PRC / 中华人民共和国国家移民管理局; older valid passports may show MPS Exit & Entry Administration / 公安部出入境管理局. If the passport prints a different authority, use the passport text. For passport type, select Ordinary / Regular / Normal only for ordinary personal passports; select Diplomatic, Official, Service, Special, Travel document, Refugee, or Other only when the document explicitly indicates that type. Official sources reviewed for this standard field rule include China National Immigration Administration certificate-issuing-authority notices and the Passport Law of the People's Republic of China; the passport biodata page remains the final source for the applicant's individual answer.

chunks.title: Official-source field answer norms
chunks.content:
Official-source field answer norms for belgium.
Use these norms only as filling guidance; if a live official form, passport, or uploaded document shows a more specific value, that document controls the answer.

Crawled source URLs: https://dofi.ibz.be/en/themes/third-country-nationals/short-stay ; https://home-affairs.ec.europa.eu/policies/schengen-borders-and-visa/visa-policy/applying-schengen-visa_en ; https://home-affairs.ec.europa.eu/document/download/5fe79baa-2d46-4b65-84ec-ea2e9b5ab1d6_en?filename=Visa+Application+Form.pdf

Passport and identity fields:
- anyone who does not provide proof of Belgian nationality.
- Provided that the applicant fulfils the entry conditions, multiple-entry visas with a long validity shall be issued for the following validity periods, unless the validity of the visa would exceed that of the travel document:
- a copy of the passport (identifying features, visa, entry and exit stamps);

Issue, expiry, and validity fields:
- The period of validity of a visa and the length of the authorised stay shall be based on the verification of entry conditions and risk assessment.
- The period of validity shall not exceed five years.
- Provided that the applicant fulfils the entry conditions, multiple-entry visas with a long validity shall be issued for the following validity periods, unless the validity of the visa would exceed that of the travel document:

Travel plan and port fields:
- One of these entry conditions is to be in possession of a valid
- an authorisation issued by a Member State or a decision taken by such State which is required with a view to entry for an intended stay in that Member State or in several Member States, or for transit through the territory of that Member State or several Member States.
- , unless the foreigner is exempt from this requirement, or unless they hold a residence permit that allows for entry without a visa or a national

Accommodation, host, and address fields:
- They must therefore present a series of documents relating to the purpose of their trip, their accommodation, and the financial and medical coverage for their trip.
- With “My Address in Belgium” (MAB) , foreign nationals can now declare their short stay address online.
- Visit the My Address in Belgium page of our website for all the details.

Purpose, occupation, work, and study fields:
- They must therefore present a series of documents relating to the purpose of their trip, their accommodation, and the financial and medical coverage for their trip.
- Supporting documents relating to the purpose of your stay, evidence of financial means and accommodation during your stay, and evidence of your intention to return to your home country after your stay.

Funds, insurance, photo, and supporting-document fields:
- They must therefore present a series of documents relating to the purpose of their trip, their accommodation, and the financial and medical coverage for their trip.
- proof that the applicant has taken out Schengen medical travel insurance valid for the entire duration of the stay, with a minimum coverage of 30,000 euros.
- Medical insurance covering emergency medical care, hospitalisation and repatriation (including in the event of death).

Answering rule: for closed-list fields, prefer the exact official option label/value. For document-backed fields, copy the passport, official identity document, application account, itinerary, invitation, accommodation, insurance, funds, or uploaded support document rather than guessing from a city, country, or prior answer.
